search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae Clinical Guidelines Virtual Management Dysuria Issue 2
site:nice.org.uk dysuria haematuria pregnancy urine culture
rejection_reasons: Treatment and medicine content was outside scope.
Patient pages were not used as exact clinical guidance.
Testing remains clinician-qualified rather than automatic.
population_applicability: Direct for people presenting with dysuria in the qualified DHA telehealth scope, with sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complication caveats.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; physical examination and in-person pathways remain partial.
UAE_applicability: Verified through the current DHA Issue 2 dysuria guideline.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports dysuria identity in DHA telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth dysuria scope and qualified applicability.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports painful or burning urination, frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain, discharge, flank pain, duration, and pregnancy or sexual context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fever with flank pain, haematuria, pregnancy, retention, recurrence, and systemic concern as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports urinalysis and qualified urine-culture documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

unresolved_source_gaps: Abdominal examination defaults, STI screen, hydration advice, fixed follow-up timing, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, and referral decisions remain unsupported.
